meta.title: Map the path before the deadline maps you.
meta.subtitle: This board turns the Wake Tech, UNC, and NC State research into a student view: what each path is, what the dates are, and what still needs to be answered.
meta.notice: Dates and rules below were checked against official pages on March 28, 2026. Re-verify before acting.

stats.label: official sources captured in the first research pass
structured Wake Tech pathway programs confirmed
active route types to keep alive at once
first major 2026 waitlist checkpoint in the current cycle

paths.title: Use the waitlist the right way
paths.description: UNC’s published guidance says waitlisted students should stay in the portal, keep grades current, and lock in a backup college instead of waiting empty-handed.
actions: Expect first-year waitlist movement only after the May 1 enrollment deadline.
Plan around a final UNC waitlist answer no later than July 31.
Do not assume extra interviews or extra recommendation letters will help.

paths.title: C-STEP is a real Wake Tech route
paths.description: UNC’s C-STEP pathway is built for students at partner community colleges, including Wake Tech, who plan an AA or AS path and keep a strong GPA.
actions: Target degree: Associate in Arts or Associate in Science.
Published application windows: April 1 and October 1.

paths.title: Wolfpack Connect creates a structured fallback
paths.description: NC State’s Wolfpack Connect is a guaranteed transfer pathway tied to all 58 North Carolina community colleges and is designed for students early in their community-college path.
actions: The current fall 2026 eligibility deadline is July 1.
Students must start with 30 transferable credits or less.
Students finish the pathway by earning an AA or AS degree aligned to an eligible major.

paths.title: Traditional transfer is still open
paths.description: Students do not need an associate degree to transfer to NC State, but they do need a clean academic plan and enough real college coursework to be competitive.
actions: NC State wants at least 24 college credits completed or in progress.
AP, IB, and CLEP do not count toward the 24-credit threshold.
Major-specific coursework matters early, especially in engineering.

destinations.name: NC State
destinations.focus: Best fit when the student is ready to plan by major, not just by school.
destinations.summary: NC State’s transfer system is practical and structured. The biggest win is that it shows students what counts by major, but the tradeoff is that major choice matters early.
whatToTrack: 24-credit transfer threshold excluding AP, IB, and CLEP
major-specific prerequisite courses and GPA notes
Wolfpack Connect fit versus traditional transfer fit
May 1 and June 15 waitlist checkpoints for the current cycle

destinations.name: UNC-Chapel Hill
destinations.focus: Strong academics still matter, but the route needs clean timing and backup discipline.
destinations.summary: UNC offers both a traditional transfer route and the C-STEP partnership path. The waitlist path is still alive in the current cycle, but it should be managed as one track among several.
whatToTrack: February 15, March 15, April 30, and May 15 transfer milestones
C-STEP eligibility rules and application windows
July 31 as the outside date for a final waitlist answer
updated grades and official transcript timing

roadmap.title: Protect current-cycle options
items: confirm every live waitlist response in the correct portal
reserve a real backup enrollment if a deposit is due
collect updated grades and final spring evidence

roadmap.title: Know where NC State stands
items: expect NC State's current waitlist answer window to close
decide whether Wake Tech fallback planning becomes primary
check Wolfpack Connect eligibility while credit counts are still low enough

roadmap.title: Close the UNC branch and commit
items: expect UNC's final waitlist answer by the end of July
lock in the academic lane for the fall
treat Wake Tech course selection as strategy, not just registration

questions: What intended major should drive the course plan from Wake Tech?
How many real college credits does the student already have, not just AP scores?
Which portal deadline matters next: May 1, June 15, or July 31?
If the favorite school does not open, which fallback is still exciting enough to pursue hard?

glossary.definition: North Carolina's Comprehensive Articulation Agreement for transfer from community colleges to UNC System schools.
UNC-Chapel Hill's Carolina Student Transfer Excellence Program for students at partner community colleges.
NC State's guaranteed transfer pathway for eligible students who begin at North Carolina community colleges.
NC State's planning tool for seeing how completed and planned coursework lines up with specific majors.
